title: Missouri Revised Statutes § 59.350 - Conveyances of personal property recorded, how
text:
59.350.  Conveyances of personal property recorded, how. — Instruments in writing, conveying chattels or personal property alone, which by any law of this state are required to be recorded or admitted of record in any recorder's office in this state, shall be recorded in a series of volumes separate from those used for recording conveyances of real estate.
(RSMo 1939 § 13163) 
Prior revisions: 1929 § 11545; 1919 § 10570; 1909 § 10383
